summary.intent: Show model probe error messages inline (not just in tooltips) on the unsupported model badge, so users can see why a model is unsupported without hovering.
summary.outcome: Fixed inline error display in both the onboarding model selector (ModelSelectCard in onboarding-view.js) and improved humanizeProbeError to preserve specific error details instead of replacing them with overly generic messages.

repo: Model probe errors flow through humanizeProbeError() in provider-validation.js before display — this function can silently replace specific errors with generic ones.
The onboarding model selector (ModelSelectCard in onboarding-view.js) and the settings page model selector (page-providers.js) are separate components requiring independent fixes.
The 'Unsupported' badge tooltip text originates from humanizeProbeError, not directly from the raw probe error logged by the backend.

code.finding: humanizeProbeError matches 'permission' too broadly — catches 'You do not have permission to access glm-5.1' and replaces it with a generic string, losing the model name. Falls through to raw error for unmatched patterns like 'temporarily unavailable'.
ModelSelectCard renders the Unsupported badge but was not showing the probe error reason inline — error was only exposed via tooltip title attribute.

workflow: When a fix is made in one component (settings page), verify if parallel components (onboarding) share the same bug — they often don't share code.
Cross-referencing HTML in the browser with log output is an effective way to identify message transformation bugs between backend error strings and frontend display.

friction: Initial fix targeted the wrong component (page-providers.js / settings page) — the user's HTML was from the onboarding flow, requiring a second pass.

The tooltip showed a stale/different error ('Service temporarily unavailable') than the current log ('no permission'), making it harder to trace whether the propagation bug was in humanizeProbeError or the component.
open_items: humanizeProbeError permission-matching pattern is still arguably too broad — the fix preserves specificity for the glm-5.1 case but the underlying pattern matching logic may need a more targeted regex to avoid future false matches.
The 'Service temporarily unavailable' error path through humanizeProbeError has no dedicated pattern and falls through to raw error — may be worth adding an explicit human-friendly mapping.
